question.short: What are the effects of sexual education programs?
question.long: What are the effects of sexual education programs on the prevention of child sexual abuse?

description: Results from studies evaluating the benefit-risk balance of sexual education programs for children aimed at preventing child sexual abuse.

description.consensus: The literature suggests that the sexual education programs evaluated mostly have positive effects on children
description.conclusion: The results of the paper suggest that the sexual education programs evaluated mostly have positive effects on children
The results of the paper suggest that the sexual education programs evaluated mostly have positive effects on children, unlike what is reported in the literature

description.consensus: The literature suggests that the sexual education programs evaluated have no significant effect on children
description.conclusion: The results of the paper show no significant effect on children of the sexual education programs evaluated
The results of the paper suggest that the sexual education programs evaluated mostly have negative effects on children, unlike what is reported in the literature

description.consensus: The literature suggests that the sexual education programs evaluated mostly have negative effects on children
description.conclusion: The results of the paper suggest that the sexual education programs evaluated mostly have negative effects on children

papers.title: Evaluation of a brief intervention for educating school children in awareness of physical and sexual abuse
papers.quote: Relative to controls, children who received the program showed an overall increase in knowledge of correct actions to take in the event of potential or actual abuse.
